description:
You are the site administrator of an on-premises application.  
Two TSV (tab-separated values) data files are already present on the system and need to be mass-processed strictly with classic **cut**, **grep**, **sort**, **paste**, and other core-utils that come pre-installed on every Linux distribution (no Python, Perl, or CSV-specific utilities).

INPUT DATA
1. /home/user/input/users_full.tsv  
   • A header line followed by one data line per user.  
   • Columns (1-indexed):  
     1 id 2 username 3 first_name 4 last_name 5 email 6 department 7 role 8 status  
2. /home/user/input/new_roles.tsv  
   • NO header line.  
   • Exactly two TAB-separated columns:  
     1 username 2 new_role  
   • It is already sorted by username and covers every username that also appears in users_full.tsv (same order).

GOAL
Generate a brand-new TSV called

/home/user/output/active_users_emails_with_new_role.tsv

that must satisfy **all** of the following:

A. It contains *only* users whose status in users_full.tsv is “ACTIVE” (case-sensitive match).  
B. Columns, in order, are  
   1 username 2 primary email address (from column 5) 3 new_role (coming from new_roles.tsv).  
C. The file has **no header** and no trailing blank lines.  
D. Fields are separated by a single TAB (U+0009).  
E. The rows must appear in the same lexical order as they were in the original input files (do **not** sort them yourself).

In addition, create a lightweight audit log documenting the operation:

/home/user/output/column_manipulation.log

Required log format (exactly three lines, in this order):

ORIGINAL_LINE_COUNT=<integer>
ACTIVE_LINE_COUNT=<integer>
FINAL_LINE_COUNT=<integer>

• ORIGINAL_LINE_COUNT = total number of **data** records in users_full.tsv (do **not** count the header).  
• ACTIVE_LINE_COUNT   = number of data records whose status field equals “ACTIVE”.  
• FINAL_LINE_COUNT    = exact number of lines written to active_users_emails_with_new_role.tsv (should equal ACTIVE_LINE_COUNT).

Both output files must be world-readable (chmod 644 or similar) but only writable by the current user.

Hints (do NOT reveal exact commands):
• Use cut to slice columns 2,5,8 out of users_full.tsv, then filter on “ACTIVE”.  
• Use cut to grab the new_role column from new_roles.tsv.  
• Because the two source files are ordered the same way, paste can be used to glue corresponding lines together without further joins.  
• Strip any unwanted columns before pasting so the final column order is as requested.  
• Generate the log file with simple line-counting commands (e.g., wc -l combined with arithmetic).

When you are done, the automated tests will:

1. Verify that /home/user/output/active_users_emails_with_new_role.tsv exists, is a plain text file, is world-readable and contains the exact three TAB-separated columns in the expected order with the correct ACTIVE users only.  
2. Verify that /home/user/output/column_manipulation.log exists, is world-readable, has exactly three lines, and that the integer values are correct and internally consistent with the contents of the first output file.
truth:
<!--
PRE-EXISTING INPUT FILES (already placed before the agent starts)

File: /home/user/input/users_full.tsv
(Each column separated by a single TAB char)
--------------------------------------------------
id	username	first_name	last_name	email	department	role	status
1001	alice	Alice	Smith	alice.smith@example.com	HR	MANAGER	ACTIVE
1002	bob	Bob	Jones	bob.jones@example.com	IT	SYSADMIN	INACTIVE
1003	carol	Carol	Taylor	carol.taylor@example.com	IT	SYSADMIN	ACTIVE
1004	dave	Dave	Wilson	dave.wilson@example.com	Finance	ACCOUNTANT	ACTIVE
1005	erin	Erin	Andrews	erin.andrews@example.com	IT	DEVELOPER	INACTIVE
--------------------------------------------------

File: /home/user/input/new_roles.tsv
(Two columns, no header, TAB-separated)
--------------------------------------------------
alice	HR-LEAD
bob	DESKTOP-ADMIN
carol	SECURITY-ADMIN
dave	SENIOR-ACCOUNTANT
erin	FRONTEND-LEAD
--------------------------------------------------

EXPECTED OUTPUT FILES (created by agent)

1. /home/user/output/active_users_emails_with_new_role.tsv
--------------------------------------------------
alice	alice.smith@example.com	HR-LEAD
carol	carol.taylor@example.com	SECURITY-ADMIN
dave	dave.wilson@example.com	SENIOR-ACCOUNTANT
--------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/output/column_manipulation.log
--------------------------------------------------
ORIGINAL_LINE_COUNT=5
ACTIVE_LINE_COUNT=3
FINAL_LINE_COUNT=3
--------------------------------------------------
-->
name: task_000000_5e191900
